title: Introduction
description: NOC 63102 – Financial sales representatives | GTR Immigration Financial sales representatives under NOC 63102 are the key advisors in Canada's world-renowned banking and investment sector. From advising on RRSPs to managing corporate investment portfolios, these TEER 2 professionals help Canadians grow and protect their wealth. For immigration, this role is a "power NOC," frequently targeted by Express Entry and OINP Human Capital Priorities due to Canada’s status as a global financial hub.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group63 – Occupations in personal serviceSub-Major Group631 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsMinor Group6310 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsNOC Code63102

duties: Sell financial products and services such as RRSPs, GICs, and mutual funds.
Advise clients on personalized investment strategies and retirement planning.
Analyze clients' financial information and objectives.
Review and update clients' portfolios based on market changes.
Solicit new business through networking and cold-calling.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business, economics, or a related field is usually required.
Licensure or certification by a recognized financial body (e.g., CSC, IFIC) is typically required.
Several years of experience in sales or a related financial service are required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Good to Very Good. Over 24,000 new job openings are projected as the financial sector expands its digital and personalized advisory services.
labour_market_demand: The demand for financial sales representatives is Very Good, especially in Toronto (Canada's financial capital) and Montreal. The complexity of modern financial products and the aging demographic's need for retirement planning have created a massive requirement for skilled advisors.
Global Hub: Work in a country with one of the most stable banking systems in the world.
High Compensation: Competitive salaries plus performance-based incentives.
Professional Prestige: A highly respected role with clear paths to Wealth Management and Investment Banking.
pathways: Federal Skilled Worker (Express Entry): A high-ranking NOC for professional advisors.
OINP Human Capital Priorities: Frequent draws for those with financial backgrounds.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Direct pathways for advisors with job offers from B.C. financial institutions.
